questions.text: Work done will be maximum if the angle between the force F and displacement d is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: (a) Incorrect. While 45° provides a decent amount of work, it's not the maximum.(b) Incorrect. When the angle is 90°, the force is completely perpendicular to the displacement, resulting in no work done.(c) Incorrect. Similar to 90°, a 180° angle leads to zero work as the force directly opposes the displacement.(d) Correct! When the angle between F and d is 0°, the force is completely aligned with the displacement, maximizing the work done (W = Fd cos θ, where θ = 0°).

questions.text: A field in which the work done in moving a body in a closed path is zero is called:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: (a) Incorrect. Work can be done in electric fields, especially if the path is not closed.(b) Correct! In a conservative field, work done around a closed path is zero because the potential energy remains constant.(c) Incorrect. While electromagnetic fields include electric and magnetic fields, the property of zero work in closed paths specifically applies to conservative fields.(d) Not entirely accurate. While gravitational fields are conservative for certain distances, work can be done within a closed path depending on the path and mass distribution.

questions.text: When a force is perpendicular to the direction of motion of the body, then work done on the body is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: (a) Correct! If the force is perpendicular (θ = 90°) to the displacement, the work done is W = Fd cos θ = 0.(b) Not always true. Work can be minimum depending on the magnitude of the force and displacement.(c) Not applicable. Work is a scalar quantity and cannot be infinite.(d) Incorrect. As mentioned above, work done is zero when the force is perpendicular to the displacement.

questions.text: Which of the following types of force does no work on the particle when it acts on it? (
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: (a) Not necessarily. Frictional force can do work against the direction of motion, like when slowing down a car.(b) Not true. Gravitational force can do work on a particle depending on its displacement, like causing it to fall.(c) Not always. Elastic forces can do work when a spring is stretched or compressed, depending on the displacement.(d) Correct! Centripetal force acts perpendicular to the direction of motion of a particle in circular motion. Since work requires a non-zero angle between force and displacement, centripetal force does no work.

questions.text: 5) If a body of mass of 2 kg is raised vertically through 2m, then the work done will be: (Work, Power & Energy, Work Done by Constant Force and Variable forces)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Reasoning: Work done (W) = Fd cos θ, where F is the force, d is the displacement, and θ is the angle between them. In this case, F = mg (weight of the body), d = 2 m (vertical distance), θ = 0° (force and displacement in the same direction), and g ≈ 9.81 m/s² (acceleration due to gravity). Therefore, W = (2 kg * 9.81 m/s²) * 2 m ≈ 39.2 J.Incorrect Options:(a) This is slightly off due to rounding errors in calculations.(b) This is a significant overestimation. It might be due to misinterpreting the units or miscalculating the force involved.(d) This is a substantial underestimation. It could be caused by confusion regarding the weight of the body or neglecting the gravitational acceleration value.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Reasoning: Average power (P) = W/t and instantaneous power is the rate of work done at any given moment. If average and instantaneous power are equal, it implies the work is being done at a constant rate, meaning the speed is not changing.Incorrect Options:(a) While power can be variable, the question specifies a condition where average and instantaneous power become equal, requiring a constant rate of work.(b) Similar to option (a), the question seeks a specific case where power is constant, not variable.(d) The rate of work itself is not specified in the question. The focus is on the uniformity of the rate, not its absolute value.

questions.text: 8. Relation Between Horsepower and Watt is given by: (Work, Power & Energy,power and energy)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Reasoning: 1 horsepower is approximately equal to 746 watts. This is a commonly used conversion factor for relating these units of power.The conversion factor of 1 horsepower (hp) being approximately equal to 746 watts is a widely accepted and commonly used relationship between these two units of power. This conversion factor facilitates the interchangeability of power measurements between the imperial system, where horsepower is frequently used, and the metric system, where watts are the standard unit of power.Incorrect Options:(a) This underestimates the conversion factor.(c) This overestimates the conversion factor, although it's a convenient approximation in some cases.(d) This is slightly off due to rounding errors and deviates from the established conversion factor.

questions.text: 9. Proton, electron, neutron, and alpha particles have the same momentum. Which of them has the highest kinetic energy (KE)?(Work, Power & Energy, law of conservation of energy)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Explanation: Kinetic energy (KE) is given by KE = 1/2 mv², where m is mass and v is velocity. Since all particles have the same momentum (p = mv), the one with the smallest mass (electron) will have the highest velocity and therefore the highest KE.Incorrect Options:(a) While protons are heavier than electrons, they also have lower velocities due to the same momentum. This leads to lower KE compared to electrons.(c) Similar to protons, neutrons have higher mass but lower velocities, resulting in lower KE than electrons.(d) Alpha particles are much heavier than electrons, but their lower velocities due to the equal momentum translate to lower KE compared to electrons.

questions.text: 10. The power output of a lamp is 6W. How much energy does the lamp give out in 2 minutes? (Work, Power & Energy,power and energy)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Explanation: Power (P) is the rate of energy transfer, measured in Joules per second (J/s). So, a 6W lamp transfers energy at a rate of 6 J/s. In 2 minutes (120 seconds), the total energy transferred would be 6 J/s * 120 s = 720 J.Incorrect Options:(a) This underestimates the energy transfer by a factor of 240. The lamp gives out energy at a much faster rate than 3 J every 2 minutes.(b) This only considers the power output for 1 minute. To calculate the energy transferred in 2 minutes, we need to multiply the power by the total time.(d) This is an arbitrary number and doesn't follow the logic of power and time.

questions.text: 11) Work done by a variable force is determined by dividing: (Work, Power & Energy, Work Done by Constant Force and Variable forces)
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: both force and displacement into small intervals
force into small and displacement into large intervals

questions.explanation: Explanation: To calculate the work done by a variable force over a finite distance, we need to account for the changes in both force and displacement along the path. Dividing only force or only displacement wouldn't provide a complete picture of the work done. By dividing both into small intervals and summing the work done in each interval, we can obtain an accurate estimate of the total work. Incorrect Options:(a) While dividing force into small intervals helps in approximating variable forces, it doesn't account for the variation in displacement, which is essential for calculating work.(b) This method only considers the path taken, but it doesn't take into account the varying force applied at different points. This leads to an incomplete picture of the work done.(d) This approach is inconsistent and doesn't provide a reliable way to calculate work done by a variable force.

questions.text: 13) The escape velocity of a body in the gravitational field of Earth is independent of: (Work, Power & Energy, escape velocity)
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: the angle at which it is thrown
both its mass and the angle at which it is thrown

questions.explanation: Explanation: Escape velocity is the minimum speed an object needs to reach to overcome Earth's gravity and escape its gravitational pull, reaching an infinite distance. It depends on only two factors:Mass of the Earth (M): A larger Earth has a stronger gravitational pull, requiring a higher escape velocity for objects to escape.Universal gravitational constant (G): This constant determines the strength of gravitational interactions and affects the escape velocity for all objects. Therefore, the escape velocity is independent of the following:(a) The mass of the object: The escape velocity is a property of the gravitational field and does not depend on the mass of the object trying to escape.(b) The angle at which it is thrown: The angle of launch only affects the trajectory of the object after reaching escape velocity, not the velocity itself. Any angle that provides sufficient speed can achieve escape.(c) Both its mass and the angle at which it is thrown: As explained above, neither the object's mass nor the launch angle influences the escape velocity.(d) The gravitational field of Earth: The question specifies the escape velocity in Earth's gravitational field. If another gravitational field were considered, the escape velocity would change due to its different mass and gravitational strength.

questions.text: The tides raise the water in the sea roughly in a day: (Work, Power & Energy, Work Done by gravitational field)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Explanation: Tides are caused by the combined gravitational pull of the Moon and the Sun on the Earth's oceans. This pull creates two bulges on opposite sides of the Earth, resulting in high tides at two locations roughly 12 hours apart. Therefore, the water in the sea experiences high tide roughly twice a day. Incorrect Options:(a) Once: The tidal cycle involves two high tides and two low tides in a day, not just one high tide.(c) Four times: While there are four tidal events in a day (two high and two low), the question specifically asks about the frequency of high tides, which is twice a day.(d) Eight times: This is completely inaccurate and has no basis in tidal mechanics.

questions.text: The source of geothermal energy is: (Work, Power & Energy,renewable energy sources)
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: decay of radioactive elements in the earth
compression of material in the earth
all as said in a, b and c

questions.explanation: Explanation: Geothermal energy comes from the Earth's internal heat, which is primarily generated from the decay of radioactive elements in the planet's core. This residual heat has been trapped since Earth's formation and continues to provide a significant source of energy. Incorrect Options:(a) Decay of radioactive elements in the earth: While this is a contributing factor, it's not the sole source. Other processes like compression and gravitational forces also contribute to Earth's internal heat.(b) Compression of material in the earth: Compression can generate heat, but it's not the primary source of geothermal energy. Radioactive decay is the predominant contributor.(d) All as said in a, b, and c: While all these factors contribute to Earth's internal heat to some extent, residual heat from radioactive decay is the dominant source of geothermal energy.

questions.text: 16) The highest value of escape velocity in the solar system is for planet: (Work, Power & Energy, escape velocity)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (c) JupiterExplanation: Escape velocity is the minimum speed needed for an object to escape a planet's gravitational pull. It depends on the planet's mass and radius. Jupiter, with its massive size, has the highest escape velocity in the solar system at approximately 60 km/s, significantly higher than other planets like Earth (11.2 km/s) or Neptune (23.6 km/s). Incorrect Options:(a) Earth: Earth's escape velocity is much lower than Jupiter's.(b) Neptune: While Neptune has a higher escape velocity than Earth, it's still significantly lower than Jupiter's.(d) Moon: The Moon's escape velocity is even lower than Earth's due to its smaller mass.

questions.text: 17) Work done by the force of friction is: (Work, Power & Energy, work done by constant and variable forces)
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: positive only for small frictional force
positive only for large frictional force

questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (b) always negativeExplanation: Friction is a non-conservative force, meaning it dissipates energy as heat. Therefore, the work done by friction is always against the direction of motion, leading to a decrease in the kinetic energy of the object. This translates to negative work done by friction. Incorrect Options:(a) Always positive: Frictional work is always negative, as it opposes movement and reduces kinetic energy.(c) Positive only for small frictional force: This statement is not true. Friction always dissipates energy regardless of its magnitude.(d) Positive only for large frictional force: Similar to option (c), this is incorrect. The direction of work done by friction is independent of its strength, and it always opposes movement.

questions.text: 18) Gravitational potential energy (PE) of a body can be found by: (Work, Power & Energy, absolute potential energy)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (b) mghExplanation: Gravitational PE depends on the mass of the object (m), the acceleration due to gravity (g), and the height of the object above a reference point (h). The formula mgh accurately calculates the gravitational PE of a body in a uniform gravitational field. The gravitational potential energy (PE) of an object near the surface of the Earth in a uniform gravitational field can be accurately calculated using the formula:PE=mghWhere:PE is the gravitational potential energy,m is the mass of the object,g is the acceleration due to gravity, andℎh is the height of the object above a reference point (typically the Earth's surface).Incorrect Options:(a) Gm/r: This formula represents the gravitational potential at a point in space due to a point mass. It doesn't consider the height of the object, which is crucial for calculating the potential energy of a specific object.(c) -Gm/r: This formula is the negative of the gravitational potential, not the PE. While negative PE can be used to express certain situations, mgh is the standard formula for calculating the gravitational PE of an object in a uniform gravitational field.(d) Both "b" and "c": While both mgh and -Gm/r have their uses in physics, they represent different concepts. Only mgh is the correct formula for calculating the gravitational PE of an object in a uniform gravitational field.

questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (d) momentum increases to 2 times and K.E increases to 4 timesExplanation:Momentum (p) is directly proportional to velocity (v): p = mv, where m is the mass of the object. Therefore, doubling the velocity doubles the momentum.doubling the velocity of an object will result in the doubling of its momentum, provided the mass remains constant. This relationship arises from the definition of momentum as the product of mass and velocity.p=mvWhere:p is the momentum of the object,m is the mass of the object,v is the velocity of the object.Kinetic energy (KE) is proportional to the square of velocity: KE = 1/2 mv². So, doubling the velocity increases KE by 2² = 4 times. Incorrect Options:(a): While momentum doubles, KE increases by four times, not two times.(b): Both momentum and KE change when velocity is doubled, so they cannot remain the same.(c): There is no constant increase in KE when velocity changes. It depends on the square of the velocity.

questions.text: Question 27: A boat moving at constant speed v through still water experiences total frictional drag F. What is the power developed by the boat? (Work, Power & Energy, power and energy)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (d) Fv Explanation:Power is the rate of work done, and work is defined as the product of force and displacement in the direction of that force.Here, the frictional drag force (F) acts against the boat's motion, but the boat maintains a constant speed. This means there is no displacement in the direction of the drag force, making the work done zero (W=F.0=0)However, the engine is still expending power to counteract the drag force, and this power can be calculated by =W/t=tF⋅0​=Fv. Therefore, the engine continuously delivers Fv power to maintain the constant speed.Incorrect Answers:(a) Fv/2​: This underestimates the power required. While it considers the force and speed, dividing by 2 is not justified in this case. The work done against the drag force directly translates to the engine's power output, and dividing by 2 introduces an unnecessary factor.(b) F v^2/2​: This relates to the kinetic energy of a moving object, which is not relevant in this scenario. The boat maintains a constant speed, not accelerating or gaining kinetic energy. This option confuses the concept of power (rate of work) with kinetic energy (total energy stored in motion).(c)Fv^2: Similar to option (b), this overestimates the power required. Squaring the velocity introduces an additional factor unrelated to the work done against the drag force. The engine's power output is directly proportional to the force it overcomes, not the square of the boat's speed.

questions.text: Question 28: When two protons are brought close together, what happens to their potential energy? (Work, Power & Energy, interconversion of potential and kinetic energy)
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: (b) P.E. increases Explanation:Like charges repel each other, meaning a force pushes them apart.As you bring the protons closer, you need to do work against this repulsive force. This work done gets stored as potential energy in the system.Therefore, the closer the protons are, the stronger the repulsion and the higher their potential energy.Incorrect Options:(a) Kinetic energy increases: This could happen if the protons collide and bounce off each other, but it doesn't have to be the case. If the protons are brought close slowly, their kinetic energy might not change significantly.(c) P.E. decreases: This is the opposite of what happens due to the repulsive force.(d) P.E. does not change: The potential energy always changes when the positions of charged particles change relative to each other unless they are infinitely far apart.

options.explanation: Here's how we can find the acceleration due to gravity (g) on the moon's surface1. Gravitational Constant:While the question doesn't explicitly provide the value, the gravitational constant (G) remains constant throughout the universe. questions.text: the particle of mass m at rest is acted upon by force p for a time t. its kinetic energy after an interval t is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Here's how we can find the kinetic energy of the particle:1. Work done by the force:The work done by the force (W) is given by the formula:W = F * dwhere:F is the magnitude of the force (given as P)d is the displacement of the particleHowever, in this case, the question specifies that the particle starts from rest. When an object starts from rest, its initial displacement is zero. Therefore, the work done by the force simplifies to:W = P * 0W = 02. Work-Energy principle:The work-energy principle states that the net work done on an object is equal to the change in its kinetic energy:W = ΔKEwhere:ΔKE is the change in kinetic energy (final kinetic energy - initial kinetic energy)Since the initial kinetic energy of the particle at rest is zero, the change in kinetic energy (ΔKE) becomes the final kinetic energy itself (KE).3. Finding the final kinetic energy:Combining the information from the above steps:0 = KE (because W = 0)This might seem like we cannot determine the kinetic energy. However, there's another approach.4. Using the relationship between force and acceleration:We know that the force acting on the particle (P) causes it to accelerate. The relationship between force, mass (m), and acceleration (a) is given by Newton's second law of motion:F = m * aRearranging this formula to solve for acceleration:a = F / m5. Relating acceleration and displacement:Although the question doesn't explicitly provide the displacement (d), we can use the relationship between acceleration, time (t), and final velocity (v_f) using the following kinematic equation:v_f = a * tSince the particle starts from rest, the initial velocity (v_i) is zero. Therefore, the final velocity (v_f) is the same as the change in velocity (Δv):Δv = v_f = a * t6. Relating kinetic energy and final velocity:The kinetic energy (KE) of the particle is related to its final velocity (v_f) by the formula:KE = 1/2 * m * v_f^27. Combining the equations:Substituting the expression for acceleration (a) from step 5 into the equation for final velocity (v_f) from step 6:v_f = (P / m) * tSquaring both sides to find the square of the final velocity (v_f^2):v_f^2 = (P^2 * t^2) / m^2Substituting this expression for v_f^2 into the formula for kinetic energy (KE) from step 6:KE = 1/2 * m * [(P^2 * t^2) / m^2]Simplifying the equation:KE = P^2 * t^2 / 2mTherefore, the kinetic energy of the particle after an interval 't' is P^2 t^2 / 2m. This matches answer choice b.

questions.text: A force of 10 N is acting on an object at an angle of 120 degrees through a distance of 4m. Calculate the work done
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Work done is given by the formula:W = F * d * cos(theta)where:W is the work doneF is the forced is the displacementtheta is the angle between the force and displacementIn this case, we have:F = 10 Nd = 4 mtheta = 120 degreesSubstituting these values into the formula, we get:W = 10 N * 4 m * cos(120 degrees)W = -20 JTherefore, the work done is -20 J.

questions.text: Which of the following fields is dependent on the path?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A conservative force is a force that does not depend on the path taken. The work done by a conservative force is independent of the path taken.A non-conservative force is a force that depends on the path taken. The work done by a non-conservative force is dependent on the path taken.Example:Gravitational force is a conservative force. The work done by gravity in moving an object from one point to another is independent of the path taken.Frictional force is a non-conservative force. The work done by friction in moving an object from one point to another is dependent on the path taken.

questions.text: Escape velocity of Jupiter is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The escape velocity of a planet is the minimum velocity that an object must have to escape the gravitational pull of the planet. It is given by the formula:v = √(2GM/R)where:v is the escape velocityG is the gravitational constantM is the mass of the planetR is the radius of the planetThe escape velocity of Jupiter is approximately 61 km/s

questions.text: In which of the following case(s) the work done be positive?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Object moves in the direction of Force
options.explanation: Work done by a force is defined as the product of the force and the displacement of the object in the direction of the force. Mathematically, it is given by:CopyW = F.d = Fd cosθwhere:W is the work doneF is the forced is the displacementθ is the angle between the force and displacementWhen the object moves in the direction of the force, the angle between the force and displacement is 0 degrees. Therefore, cosθ = 1 and the work done is positive.When the object moves in the opposite direction of the force, the angle between the force and displacement is 180 degrees. Therefore, cosθ = -1 and the work done is negative.When the object moves at a right angle to the force, the angle between the force and displacement is 90 degrees. Therefore, cosθ = 0 and the work done is zero
